items.q: Which part of the psyche operates on the reality principle?
items.why: Students confuse ego (reality) with superego (morality). The ego mediates between id's demands and reality's constraints. The superego is the internalised moral conscience.

items.correct: Every member of the target population has an equal chance of selection.
items.misconception: Just grabbing whoever is around (any haphazard method).
items.random_wrong: Picking participants who match the population profile.
items.why: Students treat 'random' as 'casual' or 'haphazard'. True randomness requires a defined sampling frame and a chance procedure (e.g., random number generator).

items.correct: Learning by observing the consequences others receive for behaviour.
items.misconception: Being directly rewarded for copying someone.
items.why: Students confuse vicarious with direct reinforcement. 'Vicarious' means experienced through someone else - you see them rewarded/punished and adjust your own behaviour accordingly.
The answer examiners want is: Bobo. “Barbie” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is NOT one of SCT's four mediating processes?
items.why: Correct answer: Punishment. “Attention” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Year of Bandura's Bobo doll study:
items.why: Exact answer: 1961. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
